primary_risks.description: Literal tool_calls anchor may dominate and produce brittle canonical behavior instead of generalized commitment.
One conversion family may over-dominate intervention rows and recreate local template coercion dynamics.
Conversion cues may narrow prompt semantics even without global rewrite pressure.
Parseability improvements could regress payload_not_object/missing_tool_calls coupling if commitment pressure is miscalibrated.
Direct scalar/text substitution may rebound under uncertainty despite structural repair exemplars.

mitigations: Deterministic contamination overlap blocking on heldout/tool_holdout
Anchor dominance telemetry with threshold checks
Behavioral monoculture telemetry with threshold checks
residual_risks: Near-miss repair may overfit wrapper normalization without semantic commitment generalization
Uncertainty-conditioned fallback exemplars may still be too sparse for robust behavior conversion
Metrics may overstate progress if gains are anchor-triggered rather than generalized

further_dataset_revision_needed_if: hard blocks pass but monoculture/anchor warnings trigger
abort_i9_direction_if: collapse-watch proxy signals indicate coercive convergence
